name: Caesar Salad
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate the peeled garlic clove in the olive oil for at least 24 hours and/or rub a wooden salad bowl with a split clove.
2. Sauté the bread cubes in 2 Tbsp of olive oil until lightly browned.
3. Break the romaine lettuce into bite-sized pieces (about 1 inch) and place it in a salad bowl.
4. Sprinkle salt, dry mustard, and freshly grated pepper over the lettuce.
5. Add chopped anchovy fillets, Worcestershire sauce, vinegar, remaining olive oil, coddled egg or raw egg yolk, lemon juice, and optional parmesan cheese to the bowl.
6. Toss all ingredients together until well combined.
7. Add the croutons on top.
For a different flavor profile, consider using crumbled blue cheese instead of Parmesan. This classic recipe features an extremely creamy and garlicky dressing, often garnished with shrimp, grilled chicken breasts, or other ingredients today.
